"input": {"filePath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/style.css", "content": "*, *::before, *::after {\n box-sizing: border-box;\n margin: 0;\n padding: 0;\n}\n\nbody {\n font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif;\n background: #f4f6f9;\n color: #333;\n line-height: 1.6;\n}\n\n.app {\n max-width: 1200px;\n margin: 0 auto;\n padding: 20px;\n}\n\nheader {\n display: flex;\n justify-content: space-between;\n align-items: center;\n margin-bottom: 24px;\n}\n\nheader h1 {\n font-size: 24px;\n font-weight: 700;\n color: #1a1a2e;\n}\n\n.header-actions {\n display: flex;\n gap: 8px;\n}\n\n.summary-cards {\n display: grid;\n grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(160px, 1fr));\n gap: 16px;\n margin-bottom: 24px;\n}\n\n.summary-card {\n background: white;\n border-radius: 10px;\n padding: 18px 20px;\n box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);\n}\n\n.summary-card .value {\n font-size: 28px;\n font-weight: 700;\n color: #1a1a2e;\n}\n\n.summary-card .label {\n font-size: 13px;\n color: #888;\n margin-top: 2px;\n}\n\n.summary-card.accent-green { border-left: 4px solid #22c55e; }\n.summary-card.accent-blue { border-left: 4px solid #3b82f6; }\n.summary-card.accent-purple { border-left: 4px solid #8b5cf6; }\n.summary-card.accent-orange { border-left: 4px solid #f59e0b; }\n.summary-card.accent-red { border-left: 4px solid #ef4444; }\n\n.toolbar {\n display: flex;\n gap: 10px;\n margin-bottom: 16px;\n flex-wrap: wrap;\n align-items: center;\n}\n\n.toolbar input[type=\"text\"],\n.toolbar select {\n padding: 8px 12px;\n border: 1px solid #d1d5db;\n border-radius: 6px;\n font-size: 14px;\n background: white;\n}\n\n.toolbar input[type=\"text\"] {\n flex: 1;\n min-width: 200px;\n}\n\n.btn {\n padding: 8px 18px;\n border: none;\n border-radius: 6px;\n font-size: 14px;\n font-weight: 500;\n cursor: pointer;\n transition: background 0.15s;\n}\n\n.btn-primary {\n background: #3b82f6;\n color: white;\n}\n.btn-primary:hover { background: #2563eb; }\n\n.btn-secondary {\n background: #e5e7eb;\n color: #333;\n}\n.btn-secondary:hover { background: #d1d5db; }\n\n.btn-danger {\n background: #ef4444;\n color: white;\n}\n.btn-danger:hover { background: #dc2626; }\n\n.btn-sm {\n padding: 5px 12px;\n font-size: 13px;\n}\n\n.btn-icon {\n background: none;\n border: 1px solid #d1d5db;\n border-radius: 6px;\n padding: 5px 10px;\n cursor: pointer;\n font-size: 13px;\n color: #555;\n}\n.btn-icon:hover { background: #f3f4f6; }\n\n.leads-section {\n background: white;\n border-radius: 10px;\n box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);\n overflow: hidden;\n}\n\ntable {\n width: 100%;\n border-collapse: collapse;\n}\n\nthead th {\n background: #f9fafb;\n text-align: left;\n padding: 12px 16px;\n font-size: 13px;\n font-weight: 600;\n color: #6b7280;\n text-transform: uppercase;\n letter-spacing: 0.5px;\n border-bottom: 2px solid #e5e7eb;\n}\n\ntbody td {\n padding: 12px 16px;\n border-bottom: 1px solid #f3f4f6;\n font-size: 14px;\n vertical-align: middle;\n}\n\ntbody tr:hover {\n background: #f9fafb;\n}\n\ntbody tr.expandable {\n cursor: pointer;\n}\n\ntbody tr.campaign-row {\n background: #f8fafc;\n}\n\ntbody tr.campaign-row td {\n padding: 8px 16px 8px 48px;\n font-size: 13px;\n}\n\n.badge {\n display: inline-block;\n padding: 2px 10px;\n border-radius: 12px;\n font-size: 12px;\n font-weight: 500;\n}\n\n.badge-active { background: #dcfce7; color: #166534; }\n.badge-inactive { background: #fee2e2; color: #991b1b; }\n\n.badge-planning { background: #fef3c7; color: #92400e; }\n.badge-progress { background: #dbeafe; color: #1e40af; }\n.badge-review { background: #e0e7ff; color: #3730a3; }\n.badge-completed { background: #dcfce7; color: #166534; }\n.badge-hold { background: #f3f4f6; color: #6b7280; }\n\n.progress-bar {\n width: 100px;\n height: 6px;\n background: #e5e7eb;\n border-radius: 3px;\n overflow: hidden;\n display: inline-block;\n vertical-align: middle;\n margin-right: 8px;\n}\n\n.progress-fill {\n height: 100%;\n background: #3b82f6;\n border-radius: 3px;\n transition: width 0.3s;\n}\n\n.progress-text {\n font-size: 12px;\n color: #6b7280;\n}\n\n.actions-cell {\n display: flex;\n gap: 6px;\n}\n\n.expand-indicator {\n display: inline-block;\n transition: transform 0.2s;\n margin-right: 8px;\n font-size: 12px;\n}\n\n.expand-indicator.open {\n transform: rotate(90deg);\n}\n\n.campaigns-subtable {\n display: none;\n}\n.campaigns-subtable.open {\n display: table-row-group;\n}\n\n.empty-state {\n text-align: center;\n padding: 48px 20px;\n color: #9ca3af;\n font-size: 15px;\n}\n\n.add-campaign-btn {\n font-size: 12px;\n padding: 3px 10px;\n margin-left: 8px;\n}\n\n.modal {\n display: none;\n position: fixed;\n inset: 0;\n background: rgba(0,0,0,0.4);\n z-index: 1000;\n justify-content: center;\n align-items: center;\n}\n\n.modal.open {\n display: flex;\n}\n\n.modal-content {\n background: white;\n border-radius: 12px;\n padding: 28px;\n width: 90%;\n max-width: 520px;\n max-height: 90vh;\n overflow-y: auto;\n position: relative;\n}\n\n.close {\n position: absolute;\n top: 16px;\n right: 20px;\n font-size: 24px;\n cursor: pointer;\n color: #9ca3af;\n}\n.close:hover { color: #333; }\n\n.modal-content h2 {\n margin-bottom: 20px;\n font-size: 18px;\n}\n\n.modal-content label {\n display: block;\n font-size: 13px;\n font-weight: 500;\n margin-top: 14px;\n margin-bottom: 4px;\n color: #374151;\n}\n\n.modal-content input:not([type=\"checkbox\"]),\n.modal-content select,\n.modal-content textarea {\n width: 100%;\n padding: 8px 12px;\n border: 1px solid #d1d5db;\n border-radius: 6px;\n font-size: 14px;\n background: white;\n}\n\n.modal-content textarea {\n resize: vertical;\n}\n\n.modal-content input[type=\"checkbox\"] {\n margin-right: 6px;\n}\n\n.form-actions {\n display: flex;\n gap: 10px;\n margin-top: 20px;\n justify-content: flex-end;\n}\n\n.toast {\n position: fixed;\n bottom: 24px;\n right: 24px;\n padding: 12px 20px;\n border-radius: 8px;\n font-size: 14px;\n color: white;\n z-index: 2000;\n opacity: 0;\n transition: opacity 0.3s, transform 0.3s;\n transform: translateY(10px);\n pointer-events: none;\n}\n\n.toast.show {\n opacity: 1;\n transform: translateY(0);\n}\n\n.toast.success { background: #22c55e; }\n.toast.error { background: #ef4444; }\n"}, "output": "Wrote file successfully.", "metadata": {"diagnostics": {}, "filepath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/style.css", "exists": false, "truncated": false}, {"total": 30510, "input": 108, "output": 3762, "reasoning": 16, "cache": {"write": 0, "read": 26624}}, "modelID": "deepseek-v4-flash-free", "providerID": "opencode", "time": {"created": 1782037452903, "completed": 1782037477042}, "finish": "tool-calls", "id": "msg_ee9b59867002IdTP57xS1ByByc", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6"}, "parts": [{"type": "step-start", "id": "msg_ee9b59867002IdTP57xS1ByByc"}, {"type": "tool", "tool": "bash", "callID": "call_00_jyWHqZBbUGi5tW26W8eR3754", "state": {"status": "completed", "input": {"description": "Install Python deps with pip3", "command": "pip3 install fastapi uvicorn sqlalchemy \"pydantic[email]\" 2>&1 | tail -5"}, "output": " \n Read more about this behavior here: \n\nnote: If you believe this is a mistake, please You can override this, at the risk of breaking your Python installation or OS, by passing --break-system-packages.\nhint: See PEP 668 for the detailed specification.\n", "metadata": {"output": " \n Read more about this behavior here: \n\nnote: If you believe this is a mistake, please contact your Python installation or OS distribution provider. You can override this, at the risk of breaking your Python installation or OS, by passing --break-system-packages.\nhint: See PEP 668 for the detailed specification.\n", "exit": 0, "description": "Install Python deps with pip3", "truncated": false}, "title": "Install Python deps with pip3", "time": {"start": 1782037456674, "end": 1782037456676}}, "id": "prt_ee9b5a1da001qt48kpLI3PMu1F", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_ee9b59867002IdTP57xS1ByByc"}, {"type": "tool", "tool": "write", "callID": "call_01_4wPtYBTLIahbfkJ4ohGd9520", "state": {"status": "completed", "input": {"filePath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/script.js", "content": "const API = 'http://127.0.0.1:8000';\n\ndocument.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {\n renderLeads();\n renderSummary();\n});\n\nasync function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {\n const res = await fetch(`${API}${path}`, {\n headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...options.headers },\n ...options,\n });\n if (res.status === 204) return null;\n const data = await res.json();\n if (!res.ok) throw new Error(data.detail || 'Request failed');\n return data;\n}\n\nfunction showToast(message, type = 'success') {\n const t = document.getElementById('toast');\n t.textContent = message;\n t.className = `toast ${type} show`;\n setTimeout(() => t.classList.remove('show'), 3000);\n}\n\nfunction openModal(id) {\n document.getElementById(id).classList.add('open');\n}\n\nfunction closeModal(id) {\n document.getElementById(id).classList.remove('open');\n}\n\nfunction formatDate(d) {\n if (!d) return '\u2014';\n const date = new Date(d);\n return date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' });\n}\n\n// --- Summary ---\nasync function renderSummary() {\n try {\n const data = await apiFetch('/api/dashboard/summary');\n const container = document.getElementById('summary');\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n
${data.total_leads}
\n
Total Team Leads
\n
\n
\n
${data.total_campaigns}
\n
Total Campaigns
\n
\n
\n
${data.active_campaigns}
\n
Active Campaigns
\n
\n
\n
${data.completed_campaigns}
\n
Completed
\n
\n
\n
${Object.keys(data.department_breakdown || {}).length}
\n
Departments
\n
\n `;\n } catch (e) {\n console.error('Summary error:', e);\n }\n}\n\n// --- Leads CRUD ---\nlet expandedLeadId = null;\n\nasync function renderLeads() {\n const search = document.getElementById('searchInput').value;\n const dept = document.getElementById('deptFilter').value;\n let url = '/api/leads?';\n if (search) url += `search=${encodeURIComponent(search)}&`;\n if (dept) url += `department=${encodeURIComponent(dept)}`;\n\n try {\n const leads = await apiFetch(url);\n const tbody = document.getElementById('leadsBody');\n const empty = document.getElementById('emptyState');\n\n if (leads.length === 0) {\n tbody.innerHTML = '';\n empty.style.display = 'block';\n return;\n }\n empty.style.display = 'none';\n\n tbody.innerHTML = leads.map(lead => `\n \n \n ▶\n ${lead.name}\n \n ${lead.email}\n ${lead.department}\n ${lead.is_active ? 'Active' : 'Inactive'}\n ${lead.campaigns ? lead.campaigns.length : '...'}\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n
\n \n \n `).join('');\n\n // Load campaigns for expanded leads\n if (expandedLeadId !== null) {\n const lead = leads.find(l => l.id === expandedLeadId);\n if (lead) {\n renderCampaignsForLead(expandedLeadId);\n }\n }\n } catch (e) {\n document.getElementById('leadsBody').innerHTML = `Error loading leads: ${e.message}`;\n }\n}\n\nfunction toggleCampaigns(leadId) {\n if (expandedLeadId === leadId) {\n expandedLeadId = null;\n } else {\n expandedLeadId = leadId;\n }\n renderLeads();\n}\n\nasync function renderCampaignsForLead(leadId) {\n const container = document.getElementById(`campaigns-content-${leadId}`);\n if (!container) return;\n try {\n const campaigns = await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns?lead_id=${leadId}`);\n const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n if (campaigns.length === 0) {\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n No campaigns assigned.\n \n
\n `;\n return;\n }\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n Campaigns for ${lead.name}\n \n
\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n ${campaigns.map(c => {\n const statusClass = c.status.toLowerCase().replace(' ', '-');\n return `\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n `;\n }).join('')}\n \n
NameTypeChannelStatusProgressDatesActions
${c.name}${c.campaign_type}${c.channel}${c.status}\n
\n ${c.progress}%\n 		${formatDate(c.start_date)} \u2014 ${formatDate(c.target_end_date)}\n \n \n
\n `;\n } catch (e) {\n container.innerHTML = `
Error loading campaigns: ${e.message}
`;\n }\n}\n\n// --- Lead Modal ---\nfunction openAddLeadModal() {\n document.getElementById('leadModalTitle').textContent = 'Add Team Lead';\n document.getElementById('leadForm').reset();\n document.getElementById('leadId').value = '';\n document.getElementById('leadActive').checked = true;\n openModal('leadModal');\n}\n\nasync function openEditLeadModal(leadId) {\n try {\n const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n document.getElementById('leadModalTitle').textContent = 'Edit Team Lead';\n document.getElementById('leadId').value = lead.id;\n document.getElementById('leadName').value = lead.name;\n document.getElementById('leadEmail').value = lead.email;\n document.getElementById('leadPhone').value = lead.phone || '';\n document.getElementById('leadDepartment').value = lead.department;\n document.getElementById('leadActive').checked = lead.is_active;\n openModal('leadModal');\n } catch (e) {\n showToast('Error loading lead: ' + e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function saveLead(event) {\n event.preventDefault();\n const id = document.getElementById('leadId').value;\n const data = {\n name: document.getElementById('leadName').value,\n email: document.getElementById('leadEmail').value,\n phone: document.getElementById('leadPhone').value,\n department: document.getElementById('leadDepartment').value,\n is_active: document.getElementById('leadActive').checked,\n };\n\n try {\n if (id) {\n await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${id}`, {\n method: 'PUT',\n body: JSON.stringify(data),\n });\n showToast('Team lead updated');\n } else {\n await apiFetch('/api/leads', {\n method: 'POST',\n body: JSON.stringify(data),\n });\n showToast('Team lead added');\n }\n closeModal('leadModal');\n renderLeads();\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function deleteLead(leadId) {\n if (!confirm('Delete this team lead and all their campaigns?')) return;\n try {\n await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`, { method: 'DELETE' });\n showToast('Team lead deleted');\n if (expandedLeadId === leadId) expandedLeadId = null;\n renderLeads();\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\n// --- Campaign CRUD ---\nfunction openAddCampaignModal(leadId) {\n document.getElementById('campaignModalTitle').textContent = 'Add Campaign';\n document.getElementById('campaignForm').reset();\n document.getElementById('campaignId').value = '';\n document.getElementById('campaignLeadId').value = leadId;\n document.getElementById('campaignStatus').value = 'Planning';\n document.getElementById('campaignProgress').value = 0;\n document.getElementById('campaignStartDate').value = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];\n openModal('campaignModal');\n}\n\nasync function openEditCampaignModal(campaignId) {\n try {\n const campaigns = await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns?lead_id=0`);\n // We need a specific fetch. Let's just get all and filter... no, let's use a different approach.\n // Actually we don't have GET /api/campaigns/{id}. Let me check... we have PUT and DELETE but not GET single campaign.\n // Let me just get all campaigns and find the one.\n const all = await apiFetch('/api/campaigns');\n const c = all.find(cam => cam.id === campaignId);\n if (!c) { showToast('Campaign not found', 'error'); return; }\n\n document.getElementById('campaignModalTitle').textContent = 'Edit Campaign';\n document.getElementById('campaignId').value = c.id;\n document.getElementById('campaignLeadId').value = c.team_lead_id;\n document.getElementById('campaignName').value = c.name;\n document.getElementById('campaignType').value = c.campaign_type;\n document.getElementById('campaignChannel').value = c.channel;\n document.getElementById('campaignDescription').value = c.description || '';\n document.getElementById('campaignStatus').value = c.status;\n document.getElementById('campaignProgress').value = c.progress;\n document.getElementById('campaignStartDate').value = c.start_date;\n document.getElementById('campaignEndDate').value = c.target_end_date || '';\n openModal('campaignModal');\n } catch (e) {\n showToast('Error loading campaign: ' + e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function saveCampaign(event) {\n event.preventDefault();\n const id = document.getElementById('campaignId').value;\n const leadId = document.getElementById('campaignLeadId').value;\n const data = {\n name: document.getElementById('campaignName').value,\n campaign_type: document.getElementById('campaignType').value,\n channel: document.getElementById('campaignChannel').value,\n description: document.getElementById('campaignDescription').value,\n status: document.getElementById('campaignStatus').value,\n progress: parseInt(document.getElementById('campaignProgress').value) || 0,\n start_date: document.getElementById('campaignStartDate').value,\n target_end_date: document.getElementById('campaignEndDate').value || null,\n team_lead_id: parseInt(leadId),\n };\n\n try {\n if (id) {\n await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns/${id}`, {\n method: 'PUT',\n body: JSON.stringify(data),\n });\n showToast('Campaign updated');\n } else {\n await apiFetch('/api/campaigns', {\n method: 'POST',\n body: JSON.stringify(data),\n });\n showToast('Campaign added');\n }\n closeModal('campaignModal');\n renderCampaignsForLead(parseInt(leadId));\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function deleteCampaign(campaignId) {\n if (!confirm('Delete this campaign?')) return;\n try {\n const all = await apiFetch('/api/campaigns');\n const c = all.find(cam => cam.id === campaignId);\n await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns/${campaignId}`, { method: 'DELETE' });\n showToast('Campaign deleted');\n if (c) renderCampaignsForLead(c.team_lead_id);\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function seedData() {\n try {\n const result = await apiFetch('/api/seed');\n showToast(result.message);\n renderLeads();\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n"}, "output": "Wrote file successfully.", "metadata": {"diagnostics": {}, "filepath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/script.js", "exists": false, "truncated": false}, "title": document.getElementById('leadActive').checked = true;\n openModal('leadModal');\n}\n\nasync function openEditLeadModal(leadId) {\n try {\n const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n document.getElementById('leadModalTitle').textContent = 'Edit Team Lead';\n document.getElementById('leadId').value = lead.id;\n document.getElementById('leadName').value = lead.name;\n document.getElementById('leadEmail').value = lead.email;\n document.getElementById('leadPhone').value = lead.phone || '';\n document.getElementById('leadDepartment').value = lead.department;\n document.getElementById('leadActive').checked = lead.is_active;\n openModal('leadModal');\n } catch (e) {\n showToast('Error loading lead: ' + e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function Let me give the user a summary of what was built and how to use it.", "time": {"start": 1782037551179, "end": 1782037551624}, "id": "prt_ee9b7184b001n8DPvHqdB3V7jc", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_ee9b71170002Up1eFdSXmlMU3Z"}, {"type": "text", "text": "All done. Here's what was built:\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\ntemp/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 backend/\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 main.py # FastAPI app \u2014 11 REST endpoints\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 models.py # TeamLead + Campaign (SQLAlchemy)\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 schemas.py # Pydantic validation schemas\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 database.py # SQLite connection\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 requirements.txt\n\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 venv/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 frontend/\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 index.html # Single-page dashboard\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 style.css # Clean, usable styling\n\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 script.js # Full CRUD via fetch API\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 start.sh\n```\n\n## What's Implemented\n\n**Data Model:**\n- **TeamLead** \u2014 name, email, phone, department, is_active, created_at (auto)\n- **Campaign** \u2014 name, type, channel, description, status (5 statuses), progress (0-100%), dates, FK to TeamLead\n\n**API Endpoints (all tested):**\n| Method | Endpoint | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| GET/POST | `/api/leads` | List (with search + dept filter) / Create lead |\n| GET/PUT/DELETE | `/api/leads/{id}` | Detail (with campaigns) / Update / Delete |\n| GET/POST | `/api/campaigns` | List (filterable by lead/status/type) / Create |\n| GET/PUT/DELETE | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Get / Update / Delete |\n| GET | `/api/dashboard/summary` | Stats: totals, active, completed, breakdowns |\n| GET | `/api/seed` | Load sample data (3 leads, 5 campaigns) |\n\n**Frontend Features:**\n- Summary cards (total leads, campaigns, active, completed, departments)\n- Leads table with expandable rows \u2192 shows campaigns inline\n- Search by name/email, filter by department\n- Modals for Add/Edit (both leads and campaigns)\n- Delete with confirmation\n- Toast notifications for success/error\n- \"Load Sample Data\" button\n- Validation (required fields, email uniqueness, progress 0-100)\n\n## How to Run\n\n**Backend** (already running on port 8000):\n```bash\ncd backend && source venv/bin/activate && uvicorn main:app --port 8000\n```\n\n**Frontend**: open `frontend/index.html` in your browser\n\n**API Docs**: http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs", "time": {"start": 1782037551625, "end": 1782037556381}, "id": "prt_ee9b71a09001P7CdJMBYibu3IA", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": need to publish the repo to github and finally we would also need to deploy our application for a live url. now usually if it's react or nextjs app i host it one vercel but now it's kind of a monorepo Explore hosting options for this FastAPI + static HTML monorepo at free cost\n\nLet me first think about the remaining tasks based on the original requirements:\n\n**Required features (minimum):** \u2705 Done\n1. \u2705 Add a new marketing team lead\n2. \u2705 View all marketing team leads\n3. \u2705 View useful details about each team lead\n4. \u2705 Update a team lead's information\n5. \u2705 Delete a team lead\n6. \u2705 Track campaigns assigned to each team lead\n7. \u2705 Track current progress/status of assigned work\n\n**Bonus features from the PDF:**\n- Search \u2705 \n- Filter by status, campaign type or department \u2014 status & campaign type filter on campaigns is done backend-side, but the UI only filters by department. Let me check the UI...\n - The UI has: search input + department filter for leads\n - But campaigns list within leads doesn't have separate status/type filters\n - Actually, campaigns are shown per-lead when you expand, so filtering by status/type could be added\n- Basic validation \u2705 (required fields, email, progress 0-100)\n- Dashboard summary \u2705 (total leads, active campaigns, etc.)\n- Better UI styling \u2705 (clean, usable)\n- Error/success messages \u2705 (toast notifications)\n- Separate campaign records from team lead records \u2705 (separate models)\n\nActually, the user says \"now we have to work on the rest of the requirements\" - but I think what they mainly want now is:\n1. Create todo.md\n2. After completing, create README.md\n3. Push to GitHub\n4. Find a free hosting solution\n\nLet me think about free hosting options:\n\nFor a **FastAPI backend + static HTML frontend** monorepo:\n\n1. **Render** (free tier): Can host the FastAPI backend. It supports Python apps directly. Free tier has some limitations (cold starts, 750 hours/month).\n \n2. **Railway** (free tier with $5 credit): Good for FastAPI backends.\n\n3. **Fly.io** (free tier): Can host FastAPI with Docker.\n\n4. **Koyeb** (free tier): Supports Python apps.\n\n5. **PythonAnywhere**: Free tier works but limited.\n\n6. **Hugging Face Spaces**: Can host Gradio or FastAPI apps for free.\n\n7. **Heroku** - no longer has free tier.\n\n8. **Vercel + serverless functions**: Could convert FastAPI to serverless functions using `vercel-python`. But wouldn't be standard FastAPI.\n\n9. **Netlify** for frontend + free backend options.\n\nActually the simplest approach:\n- **Backend**: Deploy FastAPI to Render (free tier) \u2014 just needs a `requirements.txt` and a start command\n- **Frontend**: Since it's just static HTML/JS, we can deploy it on **Vercel** or **Netlify** for free. The frontend would point to the backend URL.\n\nOR we could use **Render** for both \u2014 it can serve static files too, but we'd need to configure it properly.\n\nOR we could use a single service:\n- **Railway** \u2014 one service, can serve both the API and static files\n- **Fly.io** \u2014 one Docker container with both\n\nLet me present options to the user and let them choose.\n\nActually wait, let me re-read the user's message. They said \"now we have to work on the rest of the requirements\" and mentioned todo.md, README.md, GitHub, and deployment. They didn't specifically ask me to add more features - they want the project management stuff done.\n\nBut there are a few bonus features from the PDF that would be good to add:\n- Filter campaigns by status and campaign type in the UI (backend already supports it)\n- The UI could use some polish\n\nActually, the user said \"work on the rest of the requirements\" - let me re-read what's missing from the original PDF bonus features:\n- Search \u2705\n- Filter by status, campaign type or department \u2014 partially done. Backend supports it for both leads (department) and campaigns (status, type). Frontend only has department filter for leads.\n- Basic validation \u2705\n- Dashboard summary \u2705\n- Better UI styling \u2705\n- Error/success messages \u2705\n- Separate campaign records from team lead records \u2705\n\nSo there's actually the campaign filters that could be added to the UI (status and campaign type). But the user specifically said \"first make a todo.md file documenting our todos left\" \u2014 so let me document what's remaining and then proceed.\n\nLet me create the todo.md first, then ask about hosting preference.\n\nActually, let me think more carefully. The user wants me to:\n1. Create todo.md documenting remaining tasks\n2. Create README.md after completing\n3. Publish to GitHub\n4. Free Tier | Best For |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Render** | 750 hrs/month, 512MB RAM | FastAPI directly \u2014 just point at `main.py` |\n| **Railway** | $5 free credit/month | FastAPI + can serve static files too |\n| **Fly.io** | 3 shared VMs free | Docker-based, more configurable |\n| **Hugging Face Spaces** | Always free | Docker-based, unlimited uptime |\n| **PythonAnywhere** | Always free | Simple Python hosting, less flexible |\n\n**My recommendation:** Use **Render** for the backend (easiest FastAPI deployment, just set start command) and **Vercel/Netlify** for the frontend static files. 'Active' : 'Inactive'}\n103: ${lead.campaigns ? lead.campaigns.length : '...'}\n104: \n105: \n106: \n107: \n108: \n109: \n110: \n111:
\n112: \n113: \n114: `).join('');\n115: \n116: // Load campaigns for expanded leads\n117: if (expandedLeadId !== null) {\n118: const lead = leads.find(l => l.id === expandedLeadId);\n119: if (lead) {\n120: renderCampaignsForLead(expandedLeadId);\n121: }\n122: }\n123: } catch (e) {\n124: document.getElementById('leadsBody').innerHTML = `Error loading leads: ${e.message}`;\n125: }\n126: }\n127: \n128: function toggleCampaigns(leadId) {\n129: if (expandedLeadId === leadId) {\n130: expandedLeadId = null;\n131: } else {\n132: expandedLeadId = leadId;\n133: }\n134: renderLeads();\n135: }\n136: \n137: async function renderCampaignsForLead(leadId) {\n138: const container = document.getElementById(`campaigns-content-${leadId}`);\n139: if (!container) return;\n140: try {\n141: const campaigns = await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns?lead_id=${leadId}`);\n142: const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n143: if (campaigns.length === 0) {\n144: container.innerHTML = `\n145:

"/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/script.js", "text": "const API = 'http://127.0.0.1:8000';\n\ndocument.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {\n renderLeads();\n renderSummary();\n});\n\nasync function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {\n const res = await fetch(`${API}${path}`, {\n headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...options.headers },\n ...options,\n });\n if (res.status === 204) return null;\n const data = await res.json();\n if (!res.ok) throw new Error(data.detail || 'Request failed');\n return data;\n}\n\nfunction showToast(message, type = 'success') {\n const t = document.getElementById('toast');\n t.textContent = message;\n t.className = `toast ${type} show`;\n setTimeout(() => t.classList.remove('show'), 3000);\n}\n\nfunction openModal(id) {\n document.getElementById(id).classList.add('open');\n}\n\nfunction closeModal(id) {\n document.getElementById(id).classList.remove('open');\n}\n\nfunction formatDate(d) {\n if (!d) return '\u2014';\n const date = new Date(d);\n return date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' });\n}\n\n// --- Summary ---\nasync function renderSummary() {\n try {\n const data = await apiFetch('/api/dashboard/summary');\n const container = document.getElementById('summary');\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n
${data.total_leads}
\n
Total Team Leads
\n
\n
\n
${data.total_campaigns}
\n
Total Campaigns
\n
\n
\n
${data.active_campaigns}
\n
Active Campaigns
\n
\n
\n
${data.completed_campaigns}
\n
Completed
\n
\n
\n
${Object.keys(data.department_breakdown || {}).length}
\n
Departments
\n
\n `;\n } catch (e) {\n console.error('Summary error:', e);\n }\n}\n\n// --- Leads CRUD ---\nlet expandedLeadId = null;\n\nasync function renderLeads() {\n const search = document.getElementById('searchInput').value;\n const dept = document.getElementById('deptFilter').value;\n let url = '/api/leads?';\n if (search) url += `search=${encodeURIComponent(search)}&`;\n if (dept) url += `department=${encodeURIComponent(dept)}`;\n\n try {\n const leads = await apiFetch(url);\n const tbody = document.getElementById('leadsBody');\n const empty = document.getElementById('emptyState');\n\n if (leads.length === 0) {\n tbody.innerHTML = '';\n empty.style.display = 'block';\n return;\n }\n empty.style.display = 'none';\n\n tbody.innerHTML = leads.map(lead => `\n \n \n ▶\n ${lead.name}\n \n ${lead.email}\n ${lead.department}\n ${lead.is_active ? 'Active' : 'Inactive'}\n ${lead.campaigns ? lead.campaigns.length : '...'}\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n
\n \n \n `).join('');\n\n // Load campaigns for expanded leads\n if (expandedLeadId !== null) {\n const lead = leads.find(l => l.id === expandedLeadId);\n if (lead) {\n renderCampaignsForLead(expandedLeadId);\n }\n }\n } catch (e) {\n document.getElementById('leadsBody').innerHTML = `Error loading leads: ${e.message}`;\n }\n}\n\nfunction toggleCampaigns(leadId) {\n if (expandedLeadId === leadId) {\n expandedLeadId = null;\n } else {\n expandedLeadId = leadId;\n }\n renderLeads();\n}\n\nasync function renderCampaignsForLead(leadId) {\n const container = document.getElementById(`campaigns-content-${leadId}`);\n if (!container) return;\n try {\n const campaigns = await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns?lead_id=${leadId}`);\n const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n if (campaigns.length === 0) {\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n No campaigns assigned.\n \n
\n `;\n return;\n }\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n Campaigns for ${lead.name}\n \n
\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n ${campaigns.map(c => {\n const statusClass = c.status.toLowerCase().replace(' ', '-');\n return `\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n `;\n }).join('')}\n \n
NameTypeChannelStatusProgressDatesActions
${c.name}${c.campaign_type}${c.channel}${c.status}\n
\n ${c.progress}%\n 		${formatDate(c.start_date)} \u2014 ${formatDate(c.target_end_date)}\n \n \n
\n `;\n } catch (e) {\n container.innerHTML = `
Error loading campaigns: ${e.message}
`;\n }\n}\n\n// --- Lead Modal ---\nfunction openAddLeadModal() {\n document.getElementById('leadModalTitle').textContent = 'Add Team Lead';\n document.getElementById('leadForm').reset();\n document.getElementById('leadId').value = '';\n document.getElementById('leadActive').checked = true;\n openModal('leadModal');\n}\n\nasync function openEditLeadModal(leadId) {\n try {\n const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n document.getElementById('leadModalTitle').textContent = 'Edit Team Lead';\n document.getElementById('leadId').value = lead.id;\n document.getElementById('leadName').value = lead.name;\n document.getElementById('leadEmail').value = lead.email;\n document.getElementById('leadPhone').value = lead.phone || '';\n document.getElementById('leadDepartment').value = lead.department;\n document.getElementById('leadActive').checked = lead.is_active;\n openModal('leadModal');\n } catch (e) {\n showToast('Error loading lead: ' + e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function saveLead(event) {\n event.preventDefault();\n const id = document.getElementById('leadId').value;\n const data = {\n name: document.getElementById('leadName').value,\n email: document.getElementById('leadEmail').value,\n phone: document.getElementById('leadPhone').value,\n department: document.getElementById('leadDepartment').value,\n is_active: document.getElementById('leadActive').checked,\n };\n\n try {\n if (id) {\n await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${id}`, {\n method: 'PUT',\n body: JSON.stringify(data),\n });\n showToast('Team lead updated');\n } else {\n await apiFetch('/api/leads', {\n method: 'POST',\n body: JSON.stringify(data),\n });\n showToast('Team lead added');\n }\n closeModal('leadModal');\n renderLeads();\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function deleteLead(leadId) {\n if (!confirm('Delete this team lead and all their campaigns?')) return;\n try {\n await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`, { method: 'DELETE' });\n showToast('Team lead deleted');\n if (expandedLeadId === leadId) expandedLeadId = null;\n renderLeads();\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\n// --- Campaign CRUD ---\nfunction openAddCampaignModal(leadId) {\n document.getElementById('campaignModalTitle').textContent = 'Add Campaign';\n document.getElementById('campaignForm').reset();\n document.getElementById('campaignId').value = '';\n document.getElementById('campaignLeadId').value = leadId;\n document.getElementById('campaignStatus').value = 'Planning';\n document.getElementById('campaignProgress').value = 0;\n document.getElementById('campaignStartDate').value = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];\n openModal('campaignModal');\n}\n\nasync function openEditCampaignModal(campaignId) {\n try {\n const c = await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns/${campaignId}`);\n\n document.getElementById('campaignModalTitle').textContent = 'Edit Campaign';\n document.getElementById('campaignId').value = c.id;\n document.getElementById('campaignLeadId').value = c.team_lead_id;\n document.getElementById('campaignName').value = c.name;\n document.getElementById('campaignType').value = c.campaign_type;\n document.getElementById('campaignChannel').value = c.channel;\n document.getElementById('campaignDescription').value = c.description || '';\n document.getElementById('campaignStatus').value = c.status;\n document.getElementById('campaignProgress').value = c.progress;\n document.getElementById('campaignStartDate').value = c.start_date;\n document.getElementById('campaignEndDate').value = c.target_end_date || '';\n openModal('campaignModal');\n } catch (e) {\n showToast('Error loading campaign: ' + e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function saveCampaign(event) {\n event.preventDefault();\n const id = document.getElementById('campaignId').value;\n const leadId = document.getElementById('campaignLeadId').value;\n const data = {\n name: document.getElementById('campaignName').value,\n campaign_type: document.getElementById('campaignType').value,\n channel: document.getElementById('campaignChannel').value,\n description: document.getElementById('campaignDescription').value,\n status: document.getElementById('campaignStatus').value,\n progress: parseInt(document.getElementById('campaignProgress').value) || 0,\n start_date: document.getElementById('campaignStartDate').value,\n target_end_date: document.getElementById('campaignEndDate').value || null,\n team_lead_id: parseInt(leadId),\n };\n\n try {\n if (id) {\n await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns/${id}`, {\n method: 'PUT',\n body: JSON.stringify(data),\n });\n showToast('Campaign updated');\n } else {\n await apiFetch('/api/campaigns', {\n method: 'POST',\n body: JSON.stringify(data),\n });\n showToast('Campaign added');\n }\n closeModal('campaignModal');\n renderCampaignsForLead(parseInt(leadId));\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function deleteCampaign(campaignId) {\n if (!confirm('Delete this campaign?')) return;\n try {\n const c = await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns/${campaignId}`);\n await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns/${campaignId}`, { method: 'DELETE' });\n showToast('Campaign deleted');\n if (c) renderCampaignsForLead(c.team_lead_id);\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}\n\nasync function seedData() {\n try {\n const result = await apiFetch('/api/seed');\n showToast(result.message);\n renderLeads();\n renderSummary();\n } catch (e) {\n showToast(e.message, 'error');\n }\n}", "lineStart": 1, "lineEnd": 360, "totalLines": 360, "truncated": false}}, "title": And I need to update the HTML to include the filter dropdowns.\n\nLet me update `script.js` and `index.html` to add campaign filters within the expanded campaign section.\n\nI'll add the filters as small dropdowns in the campaign section header area.\n\nLet me update the `renderCampaignsForLead` function to include the filter dropdowns and apply them.", "time": {"start": 1782050288417, "end": 1782050289659}, "id": "prt_eea797321001hPJuBmlrWCXgGf", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea796aae002uyOb2VAKtqrqKe"}, {"type": "tool", "tool": "edit", "callID": "call_00_rrp2BfpWYioPsOQx2KVU3771", "state": {"status": "completed", "input": {"filePath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/script.js", "oldString": "async function renderCampaignsForLead(leadId) {\n const container = document.getElementById(`campaigns-content-${leadId}`);\n if (!container) return;\n try {\n const campaigns = await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns?lead_id=${leadId}`);\n const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n if (campaigns.length === 0) {\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n No campaigns assigned.\n \n
\n `;\n return;\n }\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n Campaigns for ${lead.name}\n \n
\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n ${campaigns.map(c => {\n const statusClass = c.status.toLowerCase().replace(' ', '-');\n return `\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n `;\n }).join('')}\n \n
NameTypeChannelStatusProgressDatesActions
${c.name}${c.campaign_type}${c.channel}${c.status}\n
\n ${c.progress}%\n 		${formatDate(c.start_date)} \u2014 ${formatDate(c.target_end_date)}\n \n \n
\n `;\n } catch (e) {\n container.innerHTML = `
Error loading campaigns: ${e.message}
`;\n }\n}", "newString": "async function renderCampaignsForLead(leadId) {\n const container = document.getElementById(`campaigns-content-${leadId}`);\n if (!container) return;\n try {\n const campaignStatusFilter = document.getElementById(`camp-status-${leadId}`)?.value || '';\n const campaignTypeFilter = document.getElementById(`camp-type-${leadId}`)?.value || '';\n\n let url = `/api/campaigns?lead_id=${leadId}`;\n if (campaignStatusFilter) url += `&status=${encodeURIComponent(campaignStatusFilter)}`;\n if (campaignTypeFilter) url += `&campaign_type=${encodeURIComponent(campaignTypeFilter)}`;\n\n const campaigns = await apiFetch(url);\n const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n\n const filterBar = `\n
\n Filter:\n \n \n ${campaigns.length} campaign(s)\n
\n `;\n\n if (campaigns.length === 0) {\n container.innerHTML = `\n ${filterBar}\n
\n No campaigns match your filters.\n \n
\n `;\n return;\n }\n container.innerHTML = `\n
\n Campaigns for ${lead.name}\n \n
\n ${filterBar}\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n ${campaigns.map(c => {\n const statusClass = c.status.toLowerCase().replace(' ', '-');\n return `\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n `;\n }).join('')}\n \n
NameTypeChannelStatusProgressDatesActions
${c.name}${c.campaign_type}${c.channel}${c.status}\n
\n ${c.progress}%\n 		${formatDate(c.start_date)} \u2014 ${formatDate(c.target_end_date)}\n \n \n
\n `;\n } catch (e) {\n container.innerHTML = `
Error loading campaigns: ${e.message}
`;\n }\n}"}, "output": "Edit applied successfully.", "metadata": {"diagnostics": {}, "diff": "Index: /Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/script.js\n===================================================================\n--- /Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/script.js\n+++ /Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/frontend/script.js\n@@ -137,14 +137,47 @@\n async function renderCampaignsForLead(leadId) {\n const container = document.getElementById(`campaigns-content-${leadId}`);\n if (!container) return;\n try {\n- const campaigns = await apiFetch(`/api/campaigns?lead_id=${leadId}`);\n+ const campaignStatusFilter = document.getElementById(`camp-status-${leadId}`)?.value || '';\n+ const campaignTypeFilter = document.getElementById(`camp-type-${leadId}`)?.value || '';\n+\n+ let url = `/api/campaigns?lead_id=${leadId}`;\n+ if (campaignStatusFilter) url += `&status=${encodeURIComponent(campaignStatusFilter)}`;\n+ if (campaignTypeFilter) url += `&campaign_type=${encodeURIComponent(campaignTypeFilter)}`;\n+\n+ const campaigns = await apiFetch(url);\n const lead = await apiFetch(`/api/leads/${leadId}`);\n+\n+ const filterBar = `\n+

{"type": "text", "text": "Everything is pushed to GitHub. Here's the current state:\n\n**Pushed to GitHub:** https://github.com/SucksToBeAnik/mtl-management-system-system\n\n**Deployment instructions are in `README.md`**, but here's the quick version:\n\n### Backend \u2192 Render (free)\n1. Go to https://dashboard.render.com \u2192 New Web Service\n2. Connect your GitHub repo\n3. Set **Root Directory** to `backend`\n4. Build: `pip install -r requirements.txt`\n5. Start: `uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`\n6. Deploy \u2192 get a URL like `https://mtl-management.onrender.com`\n\n### Frontend \u2192 Vercel (free)\n1. Go to https://vercel.com \u2192 Add New Project \u2192 Import GitHub repo\n2. Set **Root Directory** to `frontend`\n3. No build command needed (static files)\n4. Before deploying, add this line to `frontend/index.html` inside ``:\n ```html\n \n ```\n5. Deploy\n\nDo you have accounts on Render and Vercel already? I can guide you through each step if needed.", "time": {"start": 1782050567063, "end": 1782050569781}, "id": "prt_eea7db3970018MPah6pSoE5IE8", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "modelID": "deepseek-v4-flash-free", "providerID": "opencode", "time": {"created": 1782050843274, "completed": 1782050852280}, "finish": "tool-calls", "id": "msg_eea81ea8a002QddPlhuD1hZPmF", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6"}, "parts": [{"type": "step-start", "id": "prt_eea81f44d001b23WEfjw5U26bj", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea81ea8a002QddPlhuD1hZPmF"}, {"type": "reasoning", "text": "The error is clear - Render's Python environment is using Python 3.14, and `pydantic-core==2.23.4` which is pinned by `pydantic==2.9.2` requires building from source with Rust, but the filesystem is read-only so cargo can't build.\n\nThe issue is that:\n1. I pinned exact versions in requirements.txt that are old (fastapi==0.115.0, pydantic==2.9.2)\n2. Specifically, `pydantic==2.9.2` requires `pydantic-core==2.23.4` which has a Rust build step\n3. Render's Python 3.14 doesn't have a pre-built wheel for that old pydantic-core, so it tries to compile from source, which fails because it's read-only\n\nSolutions:\n1. **Use unpinned/fresh versions** - Remove exact version pins and let pip install the latest compatible versions which likely have pre-built wheels for Python 3.14\n2. **Pin to newer versions** that have wheels for Python 3.14\n\nThe simplest fix: unpin the versions in requirements.txt, or pin to newer versions that have pre-built wheels.\n\nLet me also update the frontend/index.html to add the backend URL.\n\nAlso, I notice the error says `Python 3.14` on Render - that's very new! Let me also update the runtime.txt to `python-3.14.0` or just remove it entirely and let Render use whatever it has.\n\nActually, the issue is that Render might be using Python 3.14 by default. Let me just fix the requirements.txt to not pin exact versions, and update runtime.txt to be less specific.", "time": {"start": 1782050846536, "end": 1782050850219}, "id": "prt_eea81f7480014wjPcn8s2SRk05", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea81ea8a002QddPlhuD1hZPmF"}, {"type": "text", "text": "The error is because `pydantic==2.9.2` pins an old `pydantic-core` that needs Rust to compile, and Render's filesystem is read-only so it can't build. The fix is to unpin the versions and let newer wheels install.", "time": {"start": 1782050850221, "end": 1782050852276}, "id": "prt_eea8205ad001fHReaqqQBHQ8VW", "sessionID": Let me check a few things:\n\n1. **Render web service** should have:\n - Root Directory: `backend`\n - Build Command: `pip install -r requirements.txt`\n - Start Command: `uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`\n\n2. **The `pydantic-core` issue**: Even with the unpinned versions, we should be fine because Render's Python 3.13 will have pre-built wheels for the latest pydantic-core. The old pinned version was the problem.\n\nBut Render showed Python 3.14 in the logs - that's very bleeding edge. The runtime.txt I set to `python-3.13` should force it to use 3.13. Actually, looking at the error again: `interpreter', '/opt/render/project/src/.venv/bin/python3.14'` - Render by default uses 3.14 now? That's concerning because pydantic-core wheels might not exist for 3.14 yet.\n\nLet me set the runtime.txt to `python-3.12` since that's more widely supported.\n\nWait, but Render might not respect runtime.txt if it doesn't support that version. Let me check... Actually, Render does respect runtime.txt. The format is `python-3.x.x`. Let me try with 3.12 which is well-supported.\n\nActually, the key issue was the pinned pydantic-core version that required a Rust build. With unpinned versions, pip will install the latest wheel. Even for Python 3.14, if there's no pre-built wheel, it would still try to build from source and fail the same way.\n\nBut Python 3.14 is very new (Jan 2026?), and pydantic-core might not have wheels for it yet on PyPI. Let me lock to python-3.12 or 3.13 which is safer.\n\nActually, I already set runtime.txt to `python-3.13`. Let me just wait for the deploy and see if it works. If not, I'll adjust further.\n\nNow, for the Vercel deployment of the frontend:\n- The user needs to go to vercel.com, import the GitHub repo\n- Set Root Directory to `frontend`\n- The `window.API_URL` is already set to `https://mtl-management-system.onrender.com`\n- No build step needed\n\nLet me also update the todo list.", "time": {"start": 1782050859103, "end": 1782050864731}, "id": "prt_eea82285f001bQv9z1tDJH53z2", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea82216f002TsAL0Z7HXJxGQg"}, {"type": "text", "text": "Pushed. The fix unpins the dependency versions so pip can install pre-built wheels instead of compiling from source, and sets Python 3.13 explicitly. Render should auto-deploy.\n\nNow let me also update the README with the correct backend URL for Vercel deployment:", "time": {"start": 1782050864733, "end": 1782050865510}, "id": "prt_eea823e5d001lLpU6xsuSmnNSM", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea82216f002TsAL0Z7HXJxGQg"}, {"type": "tool", "tool": "read", "callID": "call_00_oBYfp3svHQBQ8uKXI5Ci9001", "state": {"status": "completed", "input": {"filePath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md", "offset": 110, "limit": 30}, "output": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md\nfile\n\n110: 4. Let me just rewrite the entire file to be safe.", "time": {"start": 1782050875181, "end": 1782050875448}, "id": "prt_eea82672d001NiMxBJC5A8buOq", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea826070002CdVL7Onw7bbk12"}, {"type": "tool", "tool": "read", "callID": "call_00_j79LSwoNEZyP5Lts5CZi5344", "state": {"status": "completed", "input": {"filePath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md"}, "output": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md\nfile\n\n1: # Marketing Team Lead Management System\n2: \n3: A lightweight internal dashboard for managing marketing team leads, their assigned campaigns, and work progress. Built as a monorepo with a FastAPI backend and a vanilla JS single-page frontend.\n4: \n5: ## Tech Stack\n6: \n7: | Layer | Technology |\n8: |---|---|\n9: | **Backend** | [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/) (Python 3.12) |\n10: | **ORM** | [SQLAlchemy](https://www.sqlalchemy.org/) (sync) |\n11: | **Database** | SQLite (local) / PostgreSQL (production on Render) |\n12: | **Validation** | [Pydantic v2](https://docs.pydantic.dev/) |\n13: | **Frontend** | Vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript |\n14: | **Hosting** | Render (backend API) + Vercel (frontend) |\n15: \n16: ## Features\n17: \n18: - **Team Lead Management** \u2014 Add, view, edit, and delete marketing team leads\n19: - **Campaign Management** \u2014 Assign campaigns to leads with type, channel, status, and progress tracking\n20: - **Expandable Dashboard** \u2014 Click a lead row to expand and see their campaigns inline\n21: - **Summary Cards** \u2014 At-a-glance stats: total leads, active campaigns, completed work, department count\n22: - **Search & Filters** \u2014 Search leads by name/email, filter by department; filter campaigns by status and type\n23: - **Validation** \u2014 Required fields, email uniqueness, progress range (0\u2013100)\n24: - **Sample Data** \u2014 One-click seed button to load demo data\n25: \n26: ## Project Structure\n27: \n28: ```\n29: marketing-lead-manager/\n30: \u251c\u2500\u2500 backend/\n31: \u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 main.py # FastAPI app \u2014 API route definitions\n32: \u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 models.py # SQLAlchemy models (TeamLead, Campaign)\n33: \u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 schemas.py # Pydantic request/response schemas\n34: \u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 database.py # SQLite connection and session\n35: \u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 requirements.txt # Python dependencies\n36: \u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 Procfile # Render deployment config\n37: \u251c\u2500\u2500 frontend/\n38: \u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 index.html # Single-page dashboard UI\n39: \u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 style.css # Styling\n40: \u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 script.js # API communication and UI logic\n41: \u251c\u2500\u2500 .gitignore\n42: \u251c\u2500\u2500 runtime.txt # Python version for Render\n43: \u2514\u2500\u2500 start.sh # Local dev startup script\n44: ```\n45: \n46: ## Run Locally\n47: \n48: ### Prerequisites\n49: - Python 3.12+\n50: - pip\n51: \n52: ### Backend\n53: \n54: ```bash\n55: cd backend\n56: python3 -m venv venv\n57: source venv/bin/activate\n58: pip install -r requirements.txt\n59: uvicorn main:app --reload --port 8000\n60: ```\n61: \n62: The API will be available at `http://127.0.0.1:8000`. Interactive docs at `http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs`.\n63: \n64: ### Frontend\n65: \n66: Simply open `frontend/index.html` in your browser. The frontend is configured to point at `http://127.0.0.1:8000` by default.\n67: \n68: ### Load Sample Data\n69: \n70: Once the backend is running, click the **\"Load Sample Data\"** button on the dashboard or visit `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/seed`.\n71: \n72: ## API Endpoints\n73: \n74: | Method | Endpoint | Description |\n75: |---|---|---|\n76: | `GET` | `/api/leads` | List leads (optional `?search=` and `?department=`) |\n77: | `POST` | `/api/leads` | Create a team lead |\n78: | `GET` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Get lead details with campaigns |\n79: | `PUT` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Update a team lead |\n80: | `DELETE` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Delete a team lead |\n81: | `GET` | `/api/campaigns` | List campaigns (optional `?lead_id=`, `?status=`, `?campaign_type=`) |\n82: | `POST` | `/api/campaigns` | Create a campaign |\n83: | `GET` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Get campaign details |\n84: | `PUT` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Update a campaign |\n85: | `DELETE` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Delete a campaign |\n86: | `GET` | `/api/dashboard/summary` | Dashboard statistics |\n87: | `GET` | `/api/seed` | Load sample data |\n88: \n89: ## Deployment\n90: \n91: ### Backend (Render)\n92: \n93: 1. Push this repo to GitHub\n94: 2. Go to [Render Dashboard](https://dashboard.render.com) \u2192 New \u2192 Web Service\n95: 3. Connect your GitHub repo\n96: 4. Set:\n97: - **Root Directory**: `backend`\n98: - **Build Command**: `pip install -r requirements.txt`\n99: - **Start Command**: `uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`\n100: 5. Deploy and copy the URL (e.g. `https://your-app.onrender.com`)\n101: \n102: ### Frontend (Vercel)\n103: \n104: 1. Go to [Vercel Dashboard](https://vercel.com) \u2192 Add New \u2192 Project\n105: 2. Import your GitHub repo\n106: 3. Set:\n107: - **Root Directory**: `frontend`\n108: - **Build Command**: leave blank (static files)\n109: - **Output Directory**: `.` (root of frontend folder)\n110: 4. Before deploying, update `script.js` to point to your Render URL:\n111: \n112: ```js\n113: const API = window.API_URL || 'https://your-app.onrender.com';\n114: ```\n115: \n116: Or add before the closing `` in `index.html`:\n117: \n118: ```html\n119: \n120: ```\n121: \n122: 5. Deploy\n\n(End of file - total 122 lines)\n", "metadata": {"preview": "# Marketing Team Lead Management System\n\nA lightweight internal dashboard for managing marketing team leads, their assigned campaigns, and work progress. Built as a monorepo with a FastAPI backend and a vanilla JS single-page frontend.\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n| Layer | Technology |\n|---|---|\n| **Backend** | [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/) (Python 3.12) |\n| **ORM** | [SQLAlchemy](https://www.sqlalchemy.org/) (sync) |\n| **Database** | SQLite (local) / PostgreSQL (production on Render) |\n| **Validation** | [Pydantic v2](https://docs.pydantic.dev/) |\n| **Frontend** | Vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript |\n| **Hosting** | Render (backend API) + Vercel (frontend) |\n\n## Features\n\n- **Team Lead Management** \u2014 Add, view, edit, and delete marketing team leads\n- **Campaign Management** \u2014 Assign campaigns to leads with type, channel, status, and progress tracking\n- **Expandable Dashboard** \u2014 Click a lead row to expand and see their campaigns inline", "truncated": false, "loaded": [], "display": {"type": "file", "path": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md", "text": "# Marketing Team Lead Management System\n\nA lightweight internal dashboard for managing marketing team leads, their assigned campaigns, and work progress. Built as a monorepo with a FastAPI backend and a vanilla JS single-page frontend.\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n| Layer | Technology |\n|---|---|\n| **Backend** | [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/) (Python 3.12) |\n| **ORM** | [SQLAlchemy](https://www.sqlalchemy.org/) (sync) |\n| **Database** | SQLite (local) / PostgreSQL (production on Render) |\n| **Validation** | [Pydantic v2](https://docs.pydantic.dev/) |\n| **Frontend** | Vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript |\n| **Hosting** | Render (backend API) + Vercel (frontend) |\n\n## Features\n\n- **Team Lead Management** \u2014 Add, view, edit, and delete marketing team leads\n- **Campaign Management** \u2014 Assign campaigns to leads with type, channel, status, and progress tracking\n- **Expandable Dashboard** \u2014 Click a lead row to expand and see their campaigns inline\n- **Summary Cards** \u2014 At-a-glance stats: total leads, active campaigns, completed work, department count\n- **Search & Filters** \u2014 Search leads by name/email, filter by department; filter campaigns by status and type\n- **Validation** \u2014 Required fields, email uniqueness, progress range (0\u2013100)\n- **Sample Data** \u2014 One-click seed button to load demo data\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nmarketing-lead-manager/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 backend/\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 main.py # FastAPI app \u2014 API route definitions\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 models.py # SQLAlchemy models (TeamLead, Campaign)\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 schemas.py # Pydantic request/response schemas\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 database.py # SQLite connection and session\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 requirements.txt # Python dependencies\n\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 Procfile # Render deployment config\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 frontend/\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 index.html # Single-page dashboard UI\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 style.css # Styling\n\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 script.js # API communication and UI logic\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 .gitignore\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 runtime.txt # Python version for Render\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 start.sh # Local dev startup script\n```\n\n## Run Locally\n\n### Prerequisites\n- Python 3.12+\n- pip\n\n### Backend\n\n```bash\ncd backend\npython3 -m venv venv\nsource venv/bin/activate\npip install -r requirements.txt\nuvicorn main:app --reload --port 8000\n```\n\nThe API will be available at `http://127.0.0.1:8000`. Interactive docs at `http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs`.\n\n### Frontend\n\nSimply open `frontend/index.html` in your browser. The frontend is configured to point at `http://127.0.0.1:8000` by default.\n\n### Load Sample Data\n\nOnce the backend is running, click the **\"Load Sample Data\"** button on the dashboard or visit `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/seed`.\n\n## API Endpoints\n\n| Method | Endpoint | Description |\n|---|---|---|\n| `GET` | `/api/leads` | List leads (optional `?search=` and `?department=`) |\n| `POST` | `/api/leads` | Create a team lead |\n| `GET` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Get lead details with campaigns |\n| `PUT` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Update a team lead |\n| `DELETE` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Delete a team lead |\n| `GET` | `/api/campaigns` | List campaigns (optional `?lead_id=`, `?status=`, `?campaign_type=`) |\n| `POST` | `/api/campaigns` | Create a campaign |\n| `GET` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Get campaign details |\n| `PUT` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Update a campaign |\n| `DELETE` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Delete a campaign |\n| `GET` | `/api/dashboard/summary` | Dashboard statistics |\n| `GET` | `/api/seed` | Load sample data |\n\n## Deployment\n\n### Backend (Render)\n\n1. Push this repo to GitHub\n2. Go to [Render Dashboard](https://dashboard.render.com) \u2192 New \u2192 Web Service\n3. Connect your GitHub repo\n4. Set:\n - **Root Directory**: `backend`\n - **Build Command**: `pip install -r requirements.txt`\n - **Start Command**: `uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`\n5. Deploy and copy the URL (e.g. `https://your-app.onrender.com`)\n\n### Frontend (Vercel)\n\n1. Go to [Vercel Dashboard](https://vercel.com) \u2192 Add New \u2192 Project\n2. Import your GitHub repo\n3. Set:\n - **Root Directory**: `frontend`\n - **Build Command**: leave blank (static files)\n - **Output Directory**: `.` (root of frontend folder)\n4. Before deploying, update `script.js` to point to your Render URL:\n\n ```js\n const API = window.API_URL || 'https://your-app.onrender.com';\n ```\n\n Or add before the closing `` in `index.html`:\n\n ```html\n \n ```\n\n5. Deploy", "lineStart": 1, "lineEnd": 122, "totalLines": 122, "truncated": false}}, "title": "Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md", "time": {"start": 1782050875623, "end": 1782050875630}}, "id": "prt_eea82683a001M793h69Lzd35UA", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea826070002CdVL7Onw7bbk12"}, {"reason": "tool-calls", "type": "step-finish", "tokens": {"total": 60241, "input": 388, "output": 58, "reasoning": 19, "cache": {"write": 0, "read": 59776}}, "cost": 0, "id": "prt_eea826930001CrzgNwcaH1LNQG", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea826070002CdVL7Onw7bbk12"}]}, {"info": {"parentID": "msg_eea81ea82001dBPOhqnO7Fd9iY", "role": "assistant", "mode": "build", "agent": "build", "path": {"cwd": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp", "root": "/"}, "cost": 0, "tokens": {"total": 63129, "input": 1649, "output": 1223, "reasoning": 97, "cache": {"write": 0, "read": 60160}}, "modelID": "deepseek-v4-flash-free", "providerID": "opencode", "time": {"created": 1782050875701, "completed": 1782050884989}, "finish": "tool-calls", "id": "msg_eea826935002lh7bgFNgUTIdEl", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6"}, "parts": [{"type": "step-start", "id": "prt_eea826ec8001ngxuYntFMKhH3c", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea826935002lh7bgFNgUTIdEl"}, {"type": "reasoning", "text": "Hmm, the oldString didn't match. Let me just rewrite the entire file content.", "time": {"start": 1782050877523, "end": 1782050878559}, "id": "prt_eea827052001MdBmdh1CxAhNXW", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea826935002lh7bgFNgUTIdEl"}, {"type": "tool", "tool": "write", "callID": "call_00_fespZoEMTozorSFQuveT3202", "state": {"status": "completed", "input": {"filePath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md", "content": "# Marketing Team Lead Management System\n\nA lightweight internal dashboard for managing marketing team leads, their assigned campaigns, and work progress. Built as a monorepo with a FastAPI backend and a vanilla JS single-page frontend.\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n| Layer | Technology |\n|---|---|\n| **Backend** | [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/) (Python 3.12) |\n| **ORM** | [SQLAlchemy](https://www.sqlalchemy.org/) (sync) |\n| **Database** | SQLite (local) / PostgreSQL (production on Render) |\n| **Validation** | [Pydantic v2](https://docs.pydantic.dev/) |\n| **Frontend** | Vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript |\n| **Hosting** | Render (backend API) + Vercel (frontend) |\n\n## Features\n\n- **Team Lead Management** \u2014 Add, view, edit, and delete marketing team leads\n- **Campaign Management** \u2014 Assign campaigns to leads with type, channel, status, and progress tracking\n- **Expandable Dashboard** \u2014 Click a lead row to expand and see their campaigns inline\n- **Summary Cards** \u2014 At-a-glance stats: total leads, active campaigns, completed work, department count\n- **Search & Filters** \u2014 Search leads by name/email, filter by department; filter campaigns by status and type\n- **Validation** \u2014 Required fields, email uniqueness, progress range (0-100)\n- **Sample Data** \u2014 One-click seed button to load demo data\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nmarketing-lead-manager/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 backend/\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 main.py # FastAPI app \u2014 API route definitions\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 models.py # SQLAlchemy models (TeamLead, Campaign)\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 schemas.py # Pydantic request/response schemas\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 database.py # SQLite connection and session\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 requirements.txt # Python dependencies\n\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 Procfile # Render deployment config\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 frontend/\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 index.html # Single-page dashboard UI\n\u2502 \u251c\u2500\u2500 style.css # Styling\n\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 script.js # API communication and UI logic\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 .gitignore\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 runtime.txt # Python version for Render\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 start.sh # Local dev startup script\n```\n\n## Run Locally\n\n### Prerequisites\n- Python 3.12+\n- pip\n\n### Backend\n\n```bash\ncd backend\npython3 -m venv venv\nsource venv/bin/activate\npip install -r requirements.txt\nuvicorn main:app --reload --port 8000\n```\n\nThe API will be available at `http://127.0.0.1:8000`. Interactive docs at `http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs`.\n\n### Frontend\n\nSimply open `frontend/index.html` in your browser.\n\n### Load Sample Data\n\nOnce the backend is running, click the **\"Load Sample Data\"** button on the dashboard or visit `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/seed`.\n\n## API Endpoints\n\n| Method | Endpoint | Description |\n|---|---|---|\n| `GET` | `/api/leads` | List leads (optional `?search=` and `?department=`) |\n| `POST` | `/api/leads` | Create a team lead |\n| `GET` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Get lead details with campaigns |\n| `PUT` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Update a team lead |\n| `DELETE` | `/api/leads/{id}` | Delete a team lead |\n| `GET` | `/api/campaigns` | List campaigns (optional `?lead_id=`, `?status=`, `?campaign_type=`) |\n| `POST` | `/api/campaigns` | Create a campaign |\n| `GET` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Get campaign details |\n| `PUT` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Update a campaign |\n| `DELETE` | `/api/campaigns/{id}` | Delete a campaign |\n| `GET` | `/api/dashboard/summary` | Dashboard statistics |\n| `GET` | `/api/seed` | Load sample data |\n\n## Deployment\n\n### Backend (Render)\n\nThe backend is deployed at **https://mtl-management-system.onrender.com**.\n\nTo deploy your own instance:\n1. Fork this repo on GitHub\n2. Go to [Render Dashboard](https://dashboard.render.com) \u2192 New \u2192 Web Service\n3. Connect your GitHub repo\n4. Set:\n - **Root Directory**: `backend`\n - **Build Command**: `pip install -r requirements.txt`\n - **Start Command**: `uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`\n5. Deploy\n\n### Frontend (Vercel)\n\nThe frontend is already configured to point at the Render backend. To deploy on Vercel:\n1. Go to [Vercel Dashboard](https://vercel.com) \u2192 Add New \u2192 Project\n2. Import your GitHub repo\n3. Set:\n - **Root Directory**: `frontend`\n - **Build Command**: leave blank (static files)\n - **Output Directory**: `.` (root of frontend folder)\n4. Deploy\n"}, "output": "Wrote file successfully.", "metadata": {"diagnostics": {}, "filepath": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md", "exists": true, "truncated": false}, "title": "Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp/README.md", "time": {"start": 1782050884969, "end": 1782050884973}}, "id": "prt_eea827460001vkx2h1TDmeOnb4", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea826935002lh7bgFNgUTIdEl"}, {"reason": "tool-calls", "type": "step-finish", "tokens": {"total": 63129, "input": 1649, "output": 1223, "reasoning": 97, "cache": {"write": 0, "read": 60160}}, "cost": 0, "id": "prt_eea828d7b001CN0ZRBrWrxOlXV", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea826935002lh7bgFNgUTIdEl"}]}, {"info": {"parentID": "msg_eea81ea82001dBPOhqnO7Fd9iY", "role": "assistant", "mode": "build", "agent": "build", "path": {"cwd": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp", "root": "/"}, "cost": 0, "tokens": {"total": 63287, "input": 41, "output": 121, "reasoning": 21, "cache": {"write": 0, "read": 63104}}, "modelID": "deepseek-v4-flash-free", "providerID": "opencode", "time": {"created": 1782050884992, "completed": 1782050889674}, "finish": "tool-calls", "id": "msg_eea828d800029HWd6B846Lnr0i", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6"}, "parts": [{"type": "step-start", "id": "prt_eea829394001Up5uAG6gOFQsDg", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea828d800029HWd6B846Lnr0i"}, {"type": "reasoning", "text": "Good. Render is returning a 404 because there's no web service route configured for this URL, or the service hasn't been deployed or is in a different region or something.\n\nThis might be because:\n1. The service is still being deployed (build in progress)\n2. The service URL might be different\n\nLet me wait a bit and check again. Or maybe the user needs to configure the deployment manually on Render's dashboard.\n\nActually, the user said they tried to deploy and got the build error. The build was failing. I just pushed the fix. Render should be auto-deploying. But it might take a few minutes to build and deploy. The `no-server` header suggests there's no active server instance yet.\n\nLet me wait a bit and try again. Or I can check the build status by going to Render dashboard... but I can't browse to it.\n\nLet me just wait a minute and try again. But also, I should think about whether the fix I made will actually work.\n\nThe issue was:\n- Old `requirements.txt`: pinned exact versions, `pydantic-core==2.23.4` needed Rust to build\n- New `requirements.txt`: unpinned versions, should install pre-built wheels\n- Added `runtime.txt: python-3.13` to use a version with wheel support\n\nThis should fix it. Let me try again after a brief wait.", "time": {"start": 1782050903853, "end": 1782050907024}, "id": "prt_eea82d72d001I48mBzmSgDByrd", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea82cefe002JVSi7yOdovAqQs"}, {"type": "text", "text": "The `x-render-routing: no-server` header means the service hasn't finished deploying yet \u2014 it's still building from the latest push. "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea83d0720025114KHzbFWij6m"}, {"type": "text", "text": "Backend is live at **https://mtl-management-system.onrender.com** and fully functional.\n\n**Seeded data:** 3 leads, 5 campaigns \u2014 all endpoints working.\n\nNow for the **frontend on Vercel**, here are the exact steps:\n\n1. Go to https://vercel.com \u2192 **Add New \u2192 Project**\n2. Click **Import Git Repository** \u2192 select **SucksToBeAnik/mtl-management-system**\n3. In the **Configure Project** step:\n - **Root Directory**: click Edit and select `frontend`\n - **Build Command**: leave blank\n - **Output Directory**: leave as is\n - **Framework Preset**: choose `Other`\n4. Click **Deploy**\n\nThat's it \u2014 the frontend is already configured to point at `https://mtl-management-system.onrender.com` for the API. No additional config needed.\n\nWant me to guide through the Vercel steps in more detail?", "time": {"start": 1782050970506, "end": 1782050972279}, "id": "prt_eea83db8a001YdIsIgPc1iQbqM", "sessionID": and for white mode, --bg: #fafafa;\n --fg: #000000;\nok, lets do it and see how it goes, dont commit yet.", "id": "prt_eea98b640002WJeqOL0a8kvf3M", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea98b640001uZwmtNwfvMG4jY"}]}, {"info": {"parentID": "msg_eea98b640001uZwmtNwfvMG4jY", "role": "assistant", "mode": "build", "agent": "build", "path": {"cwd": "/Users/suckstobeanik/Downloads/temp", "root": "/"}, "cost": 0, "tokens": {"total": 68139, "input": 706, "output": 145, "reasoning": 1496, "cache": {"write": 0, "read": 65792}}, "modelID": "deepseek-v4-flash-free", "providerID": "opencode", "time": {"created": 1782052337227, "completed": 1782052356972}, "finish": "tool-calls", "id": "msg_eea98b64a002IML1hCEeleZv7G", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6"}, "parts": [{"type": "step-start", "id": "prt_eea98c036001IJjK7ZTvx8V5Yk", "sessionID": "ses_1164def0cffeO39qX3bHPu7Hq6", "messageID": "msg_eea98b64a002IML1hCEeleZv7G"}, {"type": "reasoning", "text": "The user wants a UI/UX overhaul. Let me break down the requirements:\n\n1. **Form validations** - Check if already present, add where missing\n2. **Placeholders for form inputs** - Add placeholder text\n3. **Simple chart for statistics** - Replace the number-only summary cards with a chart\n4. **Typography**: Space Mono for headings, Inter for body text\n5. **UI polish** - Make it look modern, not old school\n6. **Drop blue color** - Use dark/white only, badges can have colors\n7. **Dark mode support** - CSS variables:\n - Dark: `--bg: #121212; --fg: #ffffff;`\n - Light: `--bg: #fafafa; --fg: #000000;`\n\nLet me think about the implementation:\n\n**Validations:**\n- Currently we have: required fields, email type input, progress min/max, form submission validation\n- What's missing: proper validation error messages shown inline, phone format validation, email format validation on the client side\n- I'll add: inline error messages under fields, validate on blur/submit\n\n**Placeholders:**\n- Add placeholder attributes to all form inputs in both modals\n\n**Chart:**\n- For a simple chart without external libraries, I can use Canvas API or SVG\n- A simple bar chart or doughnut chart showing campaign status distribution would be nice\n- I'll use a canvas-based chart or pure CSS/SVG chart\n\n**Typography:**\n- Space Mono from Google Fonts for headings\n- Inter from Google Fonts for body text\n\n**UI Polish:**\n- Dark/light mode support with CSS variables\n- Better card styling, spacing, shadows\n- Remove blue, use monochrome palette\n- Modern form styling\n- Transition animations\n\n**Drop blue color:**\n- The primary button is blue (#3b82f6) - change to dark (#1a1a2e or #333)\n- Remove all blue accents, use grayscale\n- Badges can keep their color coding (green for active/completed, red for inactive/hold, etc.)\n\n**Dark mode:**\n- CSS variables approach\n- A toggle or auto-detect system preference\n- `prefers-color-scheme: dark` media query\n\nLet me plan the files to change:\n1. `index.html` - Add Google Fonts, chart container, update modals with placeholders\n2. `style.css` - Complete redesign with CSS variables, new typography, dark mode, chart styling\n3. `script.js` - Add validation logic, chart rendering, dark mode toggle\n\nThis is a significant frontend overhaul. Let me think about the structure:\n\n**Chart**: I'll create a simple donut chart using Canvas API for the campaign status distribution, and a bar chart for department breakdown. Or even simpler - a few small circular progress indicators or a horizontal bar chart using CSS.\n\nActually, for simplicity and to avoid complex canvas code, I'll create:\n- A horizontal bar chart for status distribution using pure CSS/HTML (easy to render from JS)\n- Or a simple SVG-based donut chart\n\nLet me go with a canvas-based donut chart and a CSS-based bar chart for department breakdown.\n\nFor the dark mode toggle, I'll use a simple button in the header.\n\nLet me now write out all the changes. I'll rewrite all three frontend files since there are substantial changes.\n\nActually, I should think about how to structure the chart. Let me render the chart using a canvas element in the summary section.\n\nHere's my plan for the chart:\n- A donut chart showing campaign status breakdown (Planning, In Progress, Review, Completed, On Hold)\n- Use canvas API\n\nFor the summary section, I'll keep the cards but make them look better, and add the chart below or beside them.\n\nLet me design the layout:\n- Header with title, dark mode toggle\n- Summary row: cards + chart (side by side or chart below)\n- Toolbar: search, filter, add button\n- Leads table with expandable rows\n\nLet me code this up now.\n\nFor the validation, I need to add:\n1. Inline error messages under each field\n2. Validate on input/blur\n3. Show error styling on invalid fields\n4. Prevent form submission if invalid\n\nLet me write the updated files.\n\nFor the index.html, I need to:\n- Add Google Fonts links\n- Add the dark mode toggle button\n- Update all form inputs with placeholders\n- Add error span elements for each field\n- Add the chart container\n\nFor style.css:\n- Complete rewrite with CSS variables\n- Space Mono for headings, Inter for body\n- No blue color\n- Modern card/table styling\n- Dark mode support\n- Chart styling\n- Validation error styling\n\nFor script.js:\n- Add validation functions\n- Add chart rendering\n- Add dark mode toggle\n- Update toast/notification styling\n\nLet me think about the chart more carefully. A simple approach:\n\n1. Donut chart using Canvas for campaign status\n2. Simple horizontal stacked bar showing department distribution\n\nActually, for a clean look, let me do:\n- Top section: 3-4 key metric cards (total leads, total campaigns, active, completed)\n- Below that: A donut chart for campaign status breakdown\n- Then the table\n\nLet me implement this.\n\nFor the dark mode toggle, I'll use `localStorage` to persist the preference and a `prefers-color-scheme` media query for the default.\n\nLet me start coding. I'll do the three files together.\n\nActually, I realize I should think about the chart implementation more carefully. Let me use a simple approach:\n\nFor the donut chart, I'll draw it on a canvas:\n```javascript\nfunction drawDonutChart(canvasId, data) {\n const canvas = document.getElementById(canvasId);\n const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n const colors = ['#22c55e', '#3b82f6', '#f59e0b', '#ef4444', '#8b5cf6'];\n // ... draw arcs\n}\n```\n\nBut wait, the user said \"drop the blue color\" - so I'll use a monochrome palette with just accent colors for the chart. Actually they said \"for badge we can rely on colors\" and \"drop the blue color\" - I think they mean remove blue from the primary UI (buttons, headers, etc.) but keep colors for meaningful indicators (badges, chart slices).\n\nLet me use a warm/cool neutral palette:\n- Primary: black/white/dark gray\n- Accent for chart: a set of distinct colors (green, orange, purple, red, teal, etc.)\n\nOK let me proceed with the implementation now.\n\nFor validation in forms:\n- Each input gets an error message span\n- On submit, check all fields\n- Add error class to invalid fields\n- Show error messages\n- For email: regex validation\n- For phone: optional but format check\n- For progress: 0-100 range\n- For name: min length check\n\nLet me write all the files now.", "time": {"start":
